AriaLabelingProps.description: Defines a string value that labels the current element.
Identifies the element (or elements) that labels the current element.
Identifies the element (or elements) that describes the object.
Identifies the element (or elements) that provide a detailed, extended description for the object.

AriaValidationProps.description: Identifies the element that provides an error message for the object.

AsyncLoadable.description: Whether the items are currently loading.
Handler that is called when more items should be loaded, e.g. while scrolling near the bottom.

BoxAlignmentStyleProps.description: The distribution of space around items along the main axis. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/justify-content).
The distribution of space around child items along the cross axis. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/align-content).
The alignment of children within their container. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/align-items).
The space to display between both rows and columns. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/gap).
The space to display between columns. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/column-gap).
The space to display between rows. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/row-gap).

BreadcrumbProps.description: The CSS [className](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Element/className) for the element. A function may be provided to compute the class based on component state.
A unique id for the breadcrumb, which will be passed to `onAction` when the breadcrumb is pressed.

BreadcrumbRenderProps.description: Whether the breadcrumb is for the current page.

BreadcrumbsProps.description: Handler that is called when a breadcrumb is clicked.

ButtonProps.description: The CSS [className](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Element/className) for the element. A function may be provided to compute the class based on component state.
Whether the button is in a pending state. This disables press and hover events while retaining focusability, and announces the pending state to screen readers.

ButtonRenderProps.description: Whether the button is currently hovered with a mouse.
Whether the button is currently in a pressed state.
Whether the button is focused, either via a mouse or keyboard.
Whether the button is keyboard focused.
Whether the button is currently in a pending state.

CalendarCellProps.description: The CSS [className](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Element/className) for the element. A function may be provided to compute the class based on component state.
The date to render in the cell.

CalendarCellRenderProps.description: The date that the cell represents.
The day number formatted according to the current locale.
Whether the cell is currently hovered with a mouse.
Whether the cell is currently being pressed.
Whether the cell is the first date in a range selection.
Whether the cell is the last date in a range selection.
Whether the cell is keyboard focused.

Whether the cell is disabled, according to the calendar's `minValue`, `maxValue`, and `isDisabled` props. Disabled dates are not focusable, and cannot be selected by the user. They are typically displayed with a dimmed appearance.
Whether the cell is outside the visible range of the calendar. For example, dates before the first day of a month in the same week.
Whether the cell is outside the current month.

Whether the cell is unavailable, according to the calendar's `isDateUnavailable` prop. Unavailable dates remain focusable, but cannot be selected by the user. They should be displayed with a visual affordance to indicate they are unavailable, such as a different color or a strikethrough. Note that because they are focusable, unavailable dates must meet a 4.5:1 color contrast ratio, [as defined by WCAG](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/Understanding/contrast-minimum.html).
Whether the cell is part of an invalid selection.

CalendarGridBodyProps.description: A function to render a `<CalendarCell>` for a given date.

CalendarGridHeaderProps.description: A function to render a `<CalendarHeaderCell>` for a weekday name.

CalendarGridProps.description: Either a function to render calendar cells for each date in the month, or children containing a `<CalendarGridHeader>`` and `<CalendarGridBody>` when additional customization is needed.
An offset from the beginning of the visible date range that this CalendarGrid should display. Useful when displaying more than one month at a time.
The style of weekday names to display in the calendar grid header, e.g. single letter, abbreviation, or full day name.

CalendarMonthPickerProps.description: A function to render the month picker.

CalendarProps.description: The CSS [className](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Element/className) for the element. A function may be provided to compute the class based on component state.
The amount of days that will be displayed at once. This affects how pagination works.

A function to create a new [Calendar](https://react-spectrum.adobe.com/internationalized/date/Calendar.html) object for a given calendar identifier. If not provided, the `createCalendar` function from `@internationalized/date` will be used.

CalendarYearPickerProps.description: A function to render the year picker.

CellProps.description: The CSS [className](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Element/className) for the element. A function may be provided to compute the class based on component state.
The unique id of the cell.
A string representation of the cell's contents, used for features like typeahead.
Indicates how many columns the data cell spans.

CellRenderProps.description: Whether the cell is currently in a pressed state.
Whether the cell is currently focused.
Whether the cell is currently keyboard focused.
Whether the cell is currently hovered with a mouse.
Whether the parent row is currently selected.
Whether the parent row is non-interactive, i.e. both selection and actions are disabled and the item may not be focused. Dependent on `disabledKeys` and `disabledBehavior`.
Whether keyboard focus is visible anywhere within the parent row.
The unique id of the cell.
The index of the column that this cell belongs to. Respects col spanning.
Whether the column displays hierarchical data.
Whether the parent row is expanded.
Whether the parent row has child rows.
What level the parent row has within the table.

Whether the checkbox group is read only.

Whether the checkbox group is invalid.

A ref for the HTML input element.

CheckboxRenderProps.description: Whether the checkbox is currently hovered with a mouse.
Whether the checkbox is currently in a pressed state.
Whether the checkbox is focused, either via a mouse or keyboard.
Whether the checkbox is keyboard focused.
Whether the checkbox is read only.

Collection.description: The item keys that are disabled. These items cannot be selected, focused, or otherwise interacted with.

CollectionBase.description: The item keys that are disabled. These items cannot be selected, focused, or otherwise interacted with.

CollectionBranchProps.description: The collection of items to render.
The parent node of the items to render.
A function that renders a drop indicator between items.

CollectionProps.description: Values that should invalidate the item cache when using dynamic collections.

CollectionRenderer.description: Whether this is a virtualized collection.
A delegate object that provides layout information for items in the collection.
A delegate object that provides drop targets for pointer coordinates within the collection.
A component that renders the root collection items.
A component that renders the child collection items.

CollectionRootProps.description: The collection of items to render.
A set of keys for items that should always be persisted in the DOM.
A ref to the scroll container for the collection.
A function that renders a drop indicator between items.

CollectionStateBase.description: A pre-constructed collection to use instead of building one from items and children.

ColorAreaRenderProps.description: Whether the color area is disabled.

ColorFieldProps.description: The CSS [className](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Element/className) for the element. A function may be provided to compute the class based on component state.
The color channel that this field edits. If not provided, the color is edited as a hex value.
The color space that the color field operates in if a `channel` prop is provided. If no `channel` is provided, the color field always displays the color as an RGB hex value.

Whether the color field is read only.
Whether the color field is required.
The color channel that this field edits, or "hex" if no `channel` prop is set.

ColorPickerProps.description: The children of the component. A function may be provided to alter the children based on component state.

ColorSliderProps.description: The CSS [className](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Element/className) for the element. A function may be provided to compute the class based on component state.

ColorSliderRenderProps.description: The orientation of the color slider.
Whether the color slider is disabled.

ColorSwatchPickerProps.description: The CSS [className](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Element/className) for the element. A function may be provided to compute the class based on component state.
Whether the items are arranged in a stack or grid.

ColorSwatchProps.description: The CSS [className](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Element/className) for the element. A function may be provided to compute the class based on component state.

ColorThumbProps.description: The CSS [className](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Element/className) for the element. A function may be provided to compute the class based on component state.

ColorThumbRenderProps.description: The selected color, excluding the alpha channel.
Whether this thumb is currently being dragged.
Whether the thumb is currently hovered with a mouse.
Whether the thumb is currently focused.
Whether the thumb is keyboard focused.

ColorWheelRenderProps.description: Whether the color wheel is disabled.

ColorWheelTrackProps.description: The CSS [className](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Element/className) for the element. A function may be provided to compute the class based on component state.

ColumnProps.description: The CSS [className](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Element/className) for the element. A function may be provided to compute the class based on component state.
The unique id of the column.
Whether a column is a [row header](https://www.w3.org/TR/wai-aria-1.1/#rowheader) and should be announced by assistive technology during row navigation.
A string representation of the column's contents, used for accessibility announcements.
The width of the column. This prop only applies when the `<Table>` is wrapped in a `<ResizableTableContainer>`.
The default width of the column. This prop only applies when the `<Table>` is wrapped in a `<ResizableTableContainer>`.
The minimum width of the column. This prop only applies when the `<Table>` is wrapped in a `<ResizableTableContainer>`.
The maximum width of the column. This prop only applies when the `<Table>` is wrapped in a `<ResizableTableContainer>`.

ColumnRenderProps.description: Whether the column is currently hovered with a mouse.
Whether the column is currently in a pressed state.
Whether the column is currently focused.

Whether the column is currently being resized.

ColumnResizerRenderProps.description: Whether the resizer is currently hovered with a mouse.

Whether the resizer is currently keyboard focused.

The direction that the column is currently resizable.

ComboBoxProps.description: The CSS [className](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Element/className) for the element. A function may be provided to compute the class based on component state.
The filter function used to determine if a option should be included in the combo box list.
Whether the text or key of the selected item is submitted as part of an HTML form. When `allowsCustomValue` is `true`, this option does not apply and the text is always submitted.
Whether the combo box allows the menu to be open when the collection is empty.

ComboBoxRenderProps.description: Whether the combobox is currently open.
Whether the combobox is read only.

ComboBoxValueProps.description: The CSS [className](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Element/className) for the element. A function may be provided to compute the class based on component state.

ComboBoxValueRenderProps.description: Whether the value is a placeholder.
The object values of the currently selected items.
The textValue of the currently selected items.

Whether the date field is disabled.

Whether the date field is required.

Whether an element within the date input is focused, either via a mouse or keyboard.

DialogProps.description: Children of the dialog. A function may be provided to access a function to close the dialog.

DisclosureGroupProps.description: The CSS [className](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Element/className) for the element. A function may be provided to compute the class based on component state.

The accessibility role for the disclosure's panel.

DisclosurePanelRenderProps.description: Whether keyboard focus is within the disclosure panel.

DisclosureProps.description: The CSS [className](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Element/className) for the element. A function may be provided to compute the class based on component state.
An id for the disclosure when used within a DisclosureGroup, matching the id used in `expandedKeys`.

DisclosureRenderProps.description: Whether the disclosure has keyboard focus.

DOMRenderProps.description: Overrides the default DOM element with a custom render function. This allows rendering existing components with built-in styles and behaviors such as router links, animation libraries, and pre-styled components. Requirements: - You must render the expected element type (e.g. if `<button>` is expected, you cannot render an `<a>`). - Only a single root DOM element can be rendered (no fragments). - You must pass through props and ref to the underlying DOM element, merging with your own prop as appropriate.

DragAndDrop.description: Drag and drop hooks for the collection element.

DragAndDropOptions.description: A function that returns the items being dragged. If not specified, we assume that the collection is not draggable.
A function that renders a drag preview, which is shown under the user's cursor while dragging. By default, a copy of the dragged element is rendered.

A function that renders a drop indicator element between two items in a collection. This should render a `<DropIndicator>` element. If this function is not provided, a default DropIndicator is provided.
A custom delegate object that provides drop targets for pointer coordinates within the collection.
Whether the drag and drop events should be disabled.

DragDropEvent.description: The x coordinate of the event, relative to the target element.
The y coordinate of the event, relative to the target element.

DraggableCollectionEndEvent.description: The keys of the items that were dragged.
Whether the drop ended within the same collection as it originated.

DraggableCollectionMoveEvent.description: The keys of the items that were dragged.

DraggableCollectionProps.description: Handler that is called when a drag operation is started.
Handler that is called when the drag is moved.
Handler that is called when the drag operation is ended, either as a result of a drop or a cancellation.
A function that returns the items being dragged.
The ref of the element that will be rendered as the drag preview while dragging.
Function that returns the drop operations that are allowed for the dragged items. If not provided, all drop operations are allowed.

DraggableCollectionStartEvent.description: The keys of the items that were dragged.

DropEvent.description: The drop operation that should occur.

DropIndicatorProps.description: The CSS [className](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Element/className) for the element. A function may be provided to compute the class based on component state.

DropIndicatorRenderProps.description: Whether the drop indicator is currently the active drop target.

DroppableCollectionBaseProps.description: Handler that is called when a valid drag enters a drop target.
Handler that is called after a valid drag is held over a drop target for a period of time.
Handler that is called when a valid drag exits a drop target.
Handler that is called when a valid drag is dropped on a drop target. When defined, this overrides other drop handlers such as `onInsert`, and `onItemDrop`.
A function returning the drop operation to be performed when items matching the given types are dropped on the drop target.

DroppableCollectionInsertDropEvent.description: The drop operation that should occur.

DroppableCollectionOnItemDropEvent.description: The drop operation that should occur.
Whether the drag originated within the same collection as the drop.

DroppableCollectionReorderEvent.description: The keys of the items that were reordered.
The drop operation that should occur.

DroppableCollectionRootDropEvent.description: The drop operation that should occur.

DroppableCollectionUtilityOptions.description: The drag types that the droppable collection accepts. If the collection accepts directories, include `DIRECTORY_DRAG_TYPE` in your array of allowed types.
Handler that is called when external items are dropped "between" items.
Handler that is called when external items are dropped on the droppable collection's root.
Handler that is called when items are dropped "on" an item.

Handler that is called when items are reordered within the collection. This handler only allows dropping between items, not on items. It does not allow moving items to a different parent item within a tree.
Handler that is called when items are moved within the source collection. This handler allows dropping both on or between items, and items may be moved to a different parent item within a tree.
A function returning whether a given target in the droppable collection is a valid "on" drop target for the current drag types.

DropZoneProps.description: The CSS [className](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Element/className) for the element. A function may be provided to compute the class based on component state.

DropZoneRenderProps.description: Whether the dropzone is currently hovered with a mouse.
Whether the dropzone is focused, either via a mouse or keyboard.
Whether the dropzone is keyboard focused.
Whether the dropzone is the drop target.

Expandable.description: The currently expanded keys in the collection (controlled).
The initial expanded keys in the collection (uncontrolled).
Handler that is called when items are expanded or collapsed.

FieldErrorProps.description: The CSS [className](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Element/className) for the element. A function may be provided to compute the class based on component state.
The HTML element type to render. Defaults to `'span'`. Set to `'div'` when using block-level children (e.g. `<ul>`) to avoid invalid HTML.

FileDropItem.description: The file type (usually a mime type).

FileTriggerProps.description: Specifies what mime type of files are allowed.
Whether multiple files can be selected.
Specifies the use of a media capture mechanism to capture the media on the spot.
Handler when a user selects a file.
Enables the selection of directories instead of individual files.

FlexStyleProps.description: The direction in which to layout children. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/flex-direction).
Whether to wrap items onto multiple lines. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/flex-wrap).

FocusableDOMProps.description: Whether to exclude the element from the sequential tab order. If true, the element will not be focusable via the keyboard by tabbing. This should be avoided except in rare scenarios where an alternative means of accessing the element or its functionality via the keyboard is available.

FocusableProps.description: Whether the element should receive focus on render.

FocusEvents.description: Handler that is called when the element receives focus.
Handler that is called when the element loses focus.
Handler that is called when the element's focus status changes.

FormProps.description: Whether to use native HTML form validation to prevent form submission when a field value is missing or invalid, or mark fields as required or invalid via ARIA.

GridListItemProps.description: The CSS [className](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Element/className) for the element. A function may be provided to compute the class based on component state.
The unique id of the item.
The object value that this item represents. When using dynamic collections, this is set automatically.
A string representation of the item's contents, used for features like typeahead.
Handler that is called when a user performs an action on the item. The exact user event depends on the collection's `selectionBehavior` prop and the interaction modality.

GridListItemRenderProps.description: The unique id of the item.
Whether the item's children have keyboard focus.

GridListLoadMoreItemProps.description: The load more spinner to render when loading additional items.
Whether or not the loading spinner should be rendered or not.

GridListProps.description: The CSS [className](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Element/className) for the element. A function may be provided to compute the class based on component state.
How multiple selection should behave in the collection.
The drag and drop hooks returned by `useDragAndDrop` used to enable drag and drop behavior for the GridList.
Provides content to display when there are no items in the list.
Whether the items are arranged in a stack or grid.
The primary orientation of the items. Usually this is the direction that the collection scrolls.
Which item in the collection to focus when tabbing into the collection. Overrides default roving tab index like behavior.

GridListRenderProps.description: Whether the list has no items and should display its empty state.
Whether the grid list is currently focused.
Whether the grid list is currently keyboard focused.
Whether the grid list is currently the active drop target.
Whether the items are arranged in a stack or grid.
The primary orientation of the items.

GridStyleProps.description: Defines the sizes of each row in the grid. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/grid-template-rows).
Defines the sizes of each column in the grid. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/grid-template-columns).
Defines the size of implicitly generated columns. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/grid-auto-columns).
Defines the size of implicitly generated rows. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/grid-auto-rows).
Controls how auto-placed items are flowed into the grid. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/grid-auto-flow).
Defines the default `justifySelf` for all items in the grid. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/justify-items).

GroupProps.description: The CSS [className](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Element/className) for the element. A function may be provided to compute the class based on component state.
Whether the group is read only.

An accessibility role for the group. By default, this is set to `'group'`. Use `'region'` when the contents of the group is important enough to be included in the page table of contents. Use `'presentation'` if the group is visual only and does not represent a semantic grouping of controls.

GroupRenderProps.description: Whether the group is currently hovered with a mouse.
Whether an element within the group is focused, either via a mouse or keyboard.
Whether an element within the group is keyboard focused.

HelpTextProps.description: A description for the field. Provides a hint such as specific requirements for what to choose.
An error message for the field.

HiddenDateAria.description: Props for the hidden input element.

HoverEvent.description: The type of hover event being fired.
The pointer type that triggered the hover event.
The target element of the hover event.

HoverEvents.description: Handler that is called when a hover interaction starts.
Handler that is called when a hover interaction ends.
Handler that is called when the hover state changes.

InputBase.description: Whether the input can be selected but not changed by the user.

InputDOMProps.description: The name of the input element, used when submitting an HTML form. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/input#htmlattrdefname).

The `<form>` element to associate the input with. The value of this attribute must be the id of a `<form>` in the same document. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Reference/Elements/input#form).

InputProps.description: The CSS [className](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Element/className) for the element. A function may be provided to compute the class based on component state.
Temporary text that occupies the text input when it is empty. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Reference/Attributes/placeholder).

InputRenderProps.description: Whether the input is currently hovered with a mouse.
Whether the input is focused, either via a mouse or keyboard.
Whether the input is keyboard focused.

ItemDropTarget.description: The drop position relative to the item.

ItemProps.description: Rendered contents of the item or child items.
Rendered contents of the item if `children` contains child items.
A string representation of the item's contents, used for features like typeahead.
An accessibility label for this item.
A list of child item objects. Used for dynamic collections.
Whether this item has children, even if not loaded yet.

ItemRenderProps.description: Whether the item is currently hovered with a mouse.
Whether the item is currently in a pressed state.
Whether the item is currently selected.
Whether the item is currently focused.
Whether the item is currently keyboard focused.
Whether the item is non-interactive, i.e. both selection and actions are disabled and the item may not be focused. Dependent on `disabledKeys` and `disabledBehavior`.
The type of selection that is allowed in the collection.
The selection behavior for the collection.
Whether the item is currently being dragged.
Whether the item is currently an active drop target.

KeyboardEvents.description: Handler that is called when a key is pressed.
Handler that is called when a key is released.

LabelableProps.description: The content to display as the label.

LinkDOMProps.description: A URL to link to. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/a#href).
Hints at the human language of the linked URL. See[MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/a#hreflang).
The target window for the link. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/a#target).
The relationship between the linked resource and the current page. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Attributes/rel).
Causes the browser to download the linked URL. A string may be provided to suggest a file name. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/a#download).
A space-separated list of URLs to ping when the link is followed. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/a#ping).
How much of the referrer to send when following the link. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/a#referrerpolicy).

The name of the component that triggered the popover. This is reflected on the element as the `data-trigger` attribute, and can be used to provide specific styles for the popover depending on which element triggered it.

PossibleLinkDOMRenderProps.type: (props: DetailedHTMLProps<LinkWithRequiredHref, HTMLAnchorElement> | React.JSX.IntrinsicElements[Fallback], renderProps: T) => ReactElement
PossibleLinkDOMRenderProps.description: Overrides the default DOM element with a custom render function. This allows rendering existing components with built-in styles and behaviors such as router links, animation libraries, and pre-styled components. Note: You can check if `'href' in props` in order to tell whether to render an `<a>` element. Requirements: - You must render the expected element type (e.g. if `<a>` is expected, you cannot render a `<button>`). - Only a single root DOM element can be rendered (no fragments). - You must pass through props and ref to the underlying DOM element, merging with your own prop as appropriate.

PressEvent.description: The type of press event being fired.
The pointer type that triggered the press event.
The target element of the press event.
Whether the shift keyboard modifier was held during the press event.
Whether the ctrl keyboard modifier was held during the press event.
Whether the meta keyboard modifier was held during the press event.
Whether the alt keyboard modifier was held during the press event.
X position relative to the target.
Y position relative to the target.
The key that triggered the press event, if it was triggered by a keyboard interaction. This is useful for differentiating between Space and Enter key presses.

PressEvents.description: Handler that is called when the press is released over the target.
Handler that is called when a press interaction starts.
Handler that is called when a press interaction ends, either over the target or when the pointer leaves the target.
Handler that is called when the press state changes.
Handler that is called when a press is released over the target, regardless of whether it started on the target or not.

**Not recommended – use `onPress` instead.** `onClick` is an alias for `onPress` provided for compatibility with other libraries. `onPress` provides additional event details for non-mouse interactions.

The container element in which the overlay portal will be placed. This may have unknown behavior depending on where it is portalled to.
The placement of the tooltip with respect to the trigger.

TooltipRenderProps.description: The placement of the tooltip relative to the trigger.
Whether the tooltip is currently entering. Use this to apply animations.
Whether the tooltip is currently exiting. Use this to apply animations.

TreeItemContentProps.description: The children of the component. A function may be provided to alter the children based on component state.

TreeItemProps.description: The CSS [className](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Element/className) for the element. A function may be provided to compute the class based on component state.
The unique id of the tree row.
The object value that this tree item represents. When using dynamic collections, this is set automatically.
A string representation of the tree item's contents, used for features like typeahead.
An accessibility label for this tree item.
The content of the tree item along with any nested children. Supports static nested tree items or use of a Collection to dynamically render nested tree items.
Handler that is called when a user performs an action on this tree item. The exact user event depends on the collection's `selectionBehavior` prop and the interaction modality.

TreeItemRenderProps.description: Whether the tree item is expanded.
Whether the tree item has child tree items.
What level the tree item has within the tree.
Whether the tree item's children have keyboard focus.
The unique id of the tree row.

TreeLoadMoreItemProps.description: The CSS [className](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Element/className) for the element. A function may be provided to compute the class based on component state.
The load more spinner to render when loading additional items.
Whether or not the loading spinner should be rendered or not.

TreeLoadMoreItemRenderProps.description: What level the tree item has within the tree.

TreeProps.description: The CSS [className](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Element/className) for the element. A function may be provided to compute the class based on component state.
How multiple selection should behave in the tree.
Provides content to display when there are no items in the list.
Whether `disabledKeys` applies to all interactions, or only selection.
The drag and drop hooks returned by `useDragAndDrop` used to enable drag and drop behavior for the Tree.

TreeRenderProps.description: Whether the tree has no items and should display its empty state.
Whether the tree is currently focused.
Whether the tree is currently keyboard focused.
The type of selection that is allowed in the collection.
Whether the tree is currently the active drop target.

UnsafeStyles.description: Sets the CSS [className](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Element/className) for the element. Only use as a **last resort**. Use the `style` macro via the `styles` prop instead.
Sets inline [style](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/HTMLElement/style) for the element. Only use as a **last resort**. Use the `style` macro via the `styles` prop instead.

Validation.description: Whether user input is required on the input before form submission.
Whether the input value is invalid.
Whether to use native HTML form validation to prevent form submission when the value is missing or invalid, or mark the field as required or invalid via ARIA.

A function that returns an error message if a given value is invalid. Validation errors are displayed to the user when the form is submitted if `validationBehavior="native"`. For realtime validation, use the `isInvalid` prop instead.

ValidationResult.description: Whether the input value is invalid.
The current error messages for the input if it is invalid, otherwise an empty array.
The native validation details for the input.

ValueBase.description: Handler that is called when the value changes.

ViewStyleProps.description: The Spectrum color token version number.
The background color for the element.
The width of the element's border on all four sides. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/border-width).
The width of the border on the logical start side, depending on the layout direction. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/border-inline-start-width).
The width of the border on the logical end side, depending on the layout direction. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/border-inline-end-width).
The width of the top border. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/border-top-width).
The width of the bottom border. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/border-bottom-width).
The width of the left and right borders. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/border-width).
The width of the top and bottom borders. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/border-width).
The color of the element's border on all four sides. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/border-color).
The color of the border on the logical start side, depending on the layout direction. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/border-inline-start-color).
The color of the border on the logical end side, depending on the layout direction. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/border-inline-end-color).
The color of the top border. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/border-top-color).
The color of the bottom border. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/border-bottom-color).
The color of the left and right borders. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/border-color).
The color of the top and bottom borders. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/border-width).
The border radius on all four sides of the element. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/border-radius).
The border radius for the top start corner of the element, depending on the layout direction. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/border-start-start-radius).
The border radius for the top end corner of the element, depending on the layout direction. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/border-start-end-radius).
The border radius for the bottom start corner of the element, depending on the layout direction. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/border-end-start-radius).
The border radius for the bottom end corner of the element, depending on the layout direction. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/border-end-end-radius).
The padding for all four sides of the element. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/padding).
The padding for the logical start side of the element, depending on layout direction. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/padding-inline-start).
The padding for the logical end side of an element, depending on layout direction. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/padding-inline-end).
The padding for the top side of the element. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/padding-top).
The padding for the bottom side of the element. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/padding-bottom).
The padding for both the left and right sides of the element. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/padding).
The padding for both the top and bottom sides of the element. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/padding).
Species what to do when the element's content is too long to fit its size. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/overflow).

VirtualizerProps.description: The child collection to virtualize (e.g. ListBox, GridList, or Table).
The layout object that determines the position and size of the visible elements.
